title: Hypovolemic Shock (Blood/Fluid Loss)
immediate_steps: Have the person lie flat on their back
Elevate their legs 12 inches (unless head, neck, or leg injury is suspected)
Keep them warm by covering them with a blanket or coat
Provide reassurance and keep them calm
avoid: Do not give the person anything to eat or drink, even if they complain of severe thirst
Do not elevate the head (keep it flat)
Do not use a heating pad

title: Cardiogenic Shock (Heart Failure)
immediate_steps: Have the person rest in a comfortable position (often sitting up helps them breathe better than lying flat)
Ask if they have prescribed heart medication (like nitroglycerin) and assist them in taking it
Monitor breathing and be prepared for CPR
avoid: Do not lay them flat if it makes their breathing worse
Do not let them exert themselves
Do not give them food or water
